learning_outcomes.description: read a range of passages in Ancient Greek containing the parts of speech and syntax covered in the JACT Reading Greek course.
translate into good English a range of passages covered in the JACT Reading Greek course and extracts from related authors outside of the JACT textbook.
recognise and classify a range of different parts of speech.
parse (give the grammatical properties of) various parts of speech covered in the JACT Reading Greek course.
appreciate some of the distinctive features of the studied authors in their historical and social context.

assessments.task_description:
The participation mark evaluates student preparedness demonstrated through in-class translation of set lines and active engagement in discussions directed by the lecturer. These discussions will focus on points of grammar, literary technique, and relevant literary, historical or cultural contexts. Students are encouraged to use the class time to identify and address problems encountered during preparation proactively.

Although AI and Machine translation can be used for this task, it does not help students to master the skills needed, and in some cases may be misleading and potentially more time-consuming. Nor will advanced reading, analysis and comprehension skills be developed and mastered unless students translate and understand the Greek texts yourself.

Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Homework Assignment 1: Reading Greek Grammar and Exercises based on 17A - B

Homework Assignment 2: Reading Greek Grammar and Exercises based on 17C - D

Homework Assignment 3: Translation Exercise

Homework Assignment 4: Translation and Scansion Exercise


Task: Students will be expected to complete FOUR (4) Homework Assignments containing grammar and translation exercises. Assignments are downloadable from the GREK3000 Blackboard site. The due dates are available under the assessment tab on Blackboard.

Unacknowledged use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment or unapproved learning tasks may be referred for academic misconduct. If you use AI or Machine Translation to complete assignments, you must both acknowledge this use and explain how and why AI and/or Machine Translation was used.

Use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment tasks while language learning skills are still being developed is likely to result in students failing to meet core learning outcomes for the course. Students relying on Machine Translation and AI to complete assessment are also more likely to fail in-person and other assessment tasks.



 Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course.

assessments.due_date:
5/10/2026 - 26/10/2026

Please submit your Part A: Translation and Analysis through the Turnitin submission link on Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
PART A Translation and Analysis and PART B oral presentation of a passage from Andocides' On the Mysteries

PART A: Written Component (20%)

Task: Write a detailed commentary on an allocated passage from Andocides' On the Mysteries.

Your commentary should include:

  1. A complete and accurate translation of the passages.
  2. In-depth analysis of the 5 key features identified in your oral presentation, supported by evidence from the text [approximately 200-300 words each].
  3. Submit your commentary to Turnitin by the due date.


Part B: Oral Component (5%)

Task: Present an oral translation and analysis of an allocated passage from Andocides' On the Mysteries.

  1. Recite the Greek text first, followed by your English translation.
  2. Discuss 5 key features of the passage, such as:
  3. Word choice
  4. Points of grammar
  5. Grammatical and literary constructions
  6. Historical references
  7. Historical context
  8. Legal issues
  9. Mythological references
  10. Other relevant aspects
  11. Foster class discussion by asking questions and encouraging participation.
  12. Use a PowerPoint or other visual aids to enhance your presentation if you wish.

 

Note: Please ensure that your oral and written components align and complement each other. Your commentary should provide a more in-depth analysis of the features discussed in your presentation.

 (Refer to Blackboard for passage allocations and due dates)

Unacknowledged use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment or unapproved learning tasks may be referred for academic misconduct. If you use AI or Machine Translation to complete assignments, you must both acknowledge this use and explain how and why AI and/or Machine Translation was used.

Use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment tasks while language learning skills are still being developed is likely to result in students failing to meet core learning outcomes for the course. Students relying on Machine Translation and AI to complete assessment are also more likely to fail in-person and other assessment tasks.

Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course.

assessments.task_description:
The Final Exam will contain accidence, grammatical, translation and comprehension questions, drawn from the whole course.

It will ask students to translate Seen and Unseen passages of Greek to English, answer grammatical and comprehension questions, and to complete a scansion exercise.

Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course.
